response.overview: A woman discovers a mysterious sketchbook in her home, filled with strange and unsettling drawings. As she becomes increasingly absorbed by its contents, the line between imagination and reality begins to blur, drawing her into an eerie and deeply unsettling experience.  El libro de dibujos is a short psychological horror film with a strong surreal edge. Rather than relying on overt scares, it builds tension through atmosphere, ambiguity, and the uncanny power of images. It’s the kind of story where an ordinary object becomes a doorway into something far more disturbing.  The film was written, directed, shot, and edited by Helen Eller, and that personal, auteur-driven approach is very much part of its appeal. The premise was inspired by Eller’s own childhood habit of drawing, which gives the film an intimate, almost dreamlike quality.
response.title: El libro de dibujos
